fields.description:
h2. Context  
need to create new email template

h2. Todo

1. Create dynamic template in sendgrid

    * Refer to [this design](https://www.figma.com/design/CPoA9hPdIIricEshBgDktH/Login-Wall?node-id=1181-6586&t=AJ6YMYSWARGjpgZ9-4)
    * Params:
    
        * {{whatsapp_number}} → construct [wa.me](http://wa.me) link from here
        * {{magic_link}}
        
    
2. Add template to our [email template repo](https://github.com/Match-Made/email-template-generator)
3. Add env var to fusion and populate the value in github actions w/ sendgrid Template ID (APP_EMAIL_MAGIC_LINK_TEMPLATE_ID)

h2. Acceptance Criteria

1. Magic link email template created
2. Template ID is ready to use in fusion

h2. Dependencies

1. 

    *

h2. Context  
we need to enable passwordless sign in

h2. Todo

1. Endpoint: POST /login
2. Request:  
  {  
  "email": "user@example.com",  
  "password": "string"  
  }
3. Response: { firebase user obj }
4. Hit firebase for sign in with password ([docs](https://docs.cloud.google.com/identity-platform/docs/reference/rest/v1/accounts/signInWithPassword))
5. Adjust hola_client package as well

h2. Acceptance Criteria

1. Able to login using email and password

h2. Dependencies

1. 

    *

h2. Context  
we currently hit firebase endpoint directly from FE. now, we need to have an endpoint to handle login to replace that flow

h2. Todo

1. Endpoint: POST /auth/v1/login
2. Request:  
  {  
  "email": "user@example.com",  
  "password": "string"  
  }
3. Success response:  
  {  
  "mfa_required": <APP_FT_MFA_REQUIRED> and user.mfa_required,  
  "token_id": "", → only if mfaRequired = true  
  "firebase_user": {user obj} → only if mfaRequired = false  
  }
4. Failed response:  
  {  
  "error": "",  
  "failed_count": 3  
  }
5. Details:

    * Hit hola POST /login
    
        * If APP_FT_MFA_REQUIRED = false OR user.mfa_required = false, return resp immediately (skip steps below)
        
    * On success login
    
        * Generate random token_id
        * Store firebase resp object in redis
        
            * Key: **auth:login:token:{token_id}**
            * TTL: APP_FIREBASE_USER_OBJECT_TTL (10 minutes)
            
        * Reset failed login counter **auth:login:fail:{email}**
        
    * On failed login
    
        * Get failed counter
        
            * Key: **auth:login:fail:{email}**
            
        * If counter = 10
        
            * Return 403 id blocked
            
        * If counter not exists OR counter < 10
        
            * Increment failed counter in redis
            * Reset TTL using APP_FAILED_LOGIN_COUNTER_TTL (30 minutes)
            
        * Return current failed count to FE
        * If failed count after incremented == 10:
        
            * Call RevokeRefreshTokens function to force log out
            * Send email (use APP_EMAIL_ACCOUNT_LOCKED_TEMPLATE_ID)
            * Params:
            
                * {{user_name}}
                * {{whatsapp_number}} → construct [wa.me](http://wa.me) link from here
                * {{date_time}}
                
            * Log to blacklisted_login_logs (ip addr, email)
            
        
    
6. Error codes & messages:

    * Counter >= 10: 403 - ID Blocked
    * Wrong email pass combo: 400 - Invalid Creds
    
7. Refer to [flow diagram](?tab=t.ih3v4ftggs44) for overall flow and handling both success & error cases

h2. Acceptance Criteria

1. Able to successfully login all types of user using password and email
2. Able to increment failed counter on error
3. Able to lock user and send email on 10th err
4. When FT is false returns firebase object for direct login

h2. Dependencies

1. \[FUSION\] Migration and package initialization
2. \[HOLA\] Login with email password endpoint
3. \[EMAIL-TEMPLATE\] Create account locked email template

h2. Context  
we need to generate OTP using email and be able to send them to user

h2. Todo

1. Endpoint: POST /auth/v1/otp
2. Request:  
  {  
  "email": "",  
  "phone_number": "",  
  "mfa_channel": "email",  
  "token_id": "", → optional  
  }
3. Response:  
  {  
  "token_id": "",  
  }
4. For email

    * Mfa_channel = “email”
    * Email is required
    * Phone_number is not needed
    
5. Details:

    * Enforce daily OTP limit counter
    
        * Key: **auth:otp:daily:{email}**
        * TTL: end of day
        * If >20 return 429
        * If not, increment and continue steps below
        
    * Generate OTP for email
    
        * 6-digit numeric code
        
    * If token_id is not in request, generate random token_id
    * Store OTP in redis using token_id
    
        * Key: **auth:otp:{token_id}**
        * TTL: 2 minutes
        * Overwrite existing OTP if any to invalidate old ones
        
    * Send OTP to email (use APP_EMAIL_LOGIN_OTP_TEMPLATE_ID)
    
        * Params:
        
            * {{user_name}}
            * {{otp_code}}
            * {{whatsapp_number}} → construct [wa.me](http://wa.me) link from here
            
        
    * Return token_id
    
6. This task should only handle email
7. Make sure that the c.iii functions are reusable so that we can use them in login with email pass endpoint

h2. Acceptance Criteria

1. Able to generate OTP and send them to user
2. Able to enforce 20 OTP per day per account limit

h2. Dependencies

1. \[FUSION\] Migration and package initialization
2. \[EMAIL-TEMPLATE\] Create login OTP email template

h2. Context  
we need to send OTP to user whatsapp

h2. Todo

1. Request:  
  {  
  "email": "",  
  "phone_number": "",  
  "mfa_channel": "whatsapp"  
  }
2. For whatsapp

    * Mfa_channel = “whatsapp”
    * Phone_number is required
    * Email is not needed
    
3. Flow is similar to email, but send the OTP to whatsapp using XXX
4. Adjust generate OTP endpoint depending on SPIKE result

h2. Acceptance Criteria

1. Able to send OTP using chosen WhatsApp Business provider

h2. Dependencies

1. \[SPIKE\] WhatsApp Business Providers
2. \[FUSION\]\[DELIVERY/USER\] Generate OTP endpoint (email)

fields.summary: [FUSION] Send OTP on login with email password endpoint
fields.description:
h2. Context  
we need to adjust login with email password endpoint to also generate and send OTP on success

h2. Todo

1. On success login

    * Use previously created functions to generate and send OTP before returning
    * Always use email first after login
    

h2. Acceptance Criteria

1. Able to generate OTP and send them to user when login using valid email/pass

h2. Dependencies

1. \[FUSION\] Generate OTP endpoint (email)
2. \[FUSION\] Login with email password endpoint

### Context

we need to be able to verify OTPs based on previously generated ones

### Todo

* Endpoint: POST /auth/v1/login/verify-otp
* Request:

```json
{
  "token_id": "",
  "otp": "123456",
  "mfa_channel": "email"/"whatsapp"
}
```

* Response:

```json
{
  "kind": "",
  "localId": "",
  "email": "",
  "displayName": "",
  "idToken": "",
  "registered": "",
  "refreshToken": "",
  "expiresIn": ""
}
```

* Details:

    * Retrieve OTP
    
        * Key: `auth:otp:{token_id}`
        
    * Verify
    
        * Check OTP matches stored OTP
        
    * Get stored user obj
    
        * Key: `auth:login:token:{token_id}`
        
    * On success
    
        * Delete:
        
            * Previously fetched `auth:otp:{token_id}`
            * All `auth:login:\*` (token and fail counter)
            
        * If mfa_channel = "whatsapp", send email (use APP_EMAIL_SUCCESSFUL_LOGIN_TEMPLATE_ID)
        
            * Params: `{{user_name}}`, `{{whatsapp_number}}`, `{{date_time}}`, `{{location}}`, `{{device_type}}`, `{{ip_address}}`, `{{reset_password_link}}`
            
        * Return response object
        
    
* Make sure the verification functions are reusable for update phone number flows

### Acceptance Criteria

* Able to verify OTP

### Dependencies

* \[FUSION\] Migration and package initialization
* \[EMAIL-TEMPLATE\] Create successful login email template

2. Should consist:

    * Error states
    * Microinteractions (hover, disable, etc)
    * Request new OTP timer (2 minutes) after clicking
    
3. Does not consist:

    * Endpoint integration
    * Flow integration (create a temporary button in login modal for QA testing)
    
4. Use [shadcn input OTP](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/radix/input-otp)
5. For contact support use VITE_WHATSAPP_NUMBER

h2. Context  
need to adjust the behavior of our login page to hit BE instead of directly hitting firebase

h2. Todo

1. Implement FT to protect route VITE_FT_LOGIN_IMPROVEMENT

    * If false, use the current flow
    * If true, proceed with new flow
    
2. Hit POST /auth/v1/login

    * On error, show labels/toast
    * On success:
    
        * If mfaRequired = true redirect to OTP page
        * Else redirect to dashboard and set user
        
    
3. Hit POST /auth/v1/otp when clicking request new OTP
4. Hit POST /auth/v1/login/verify-otp

    * Set user based on response
    
5. Adjust user object storage and ensure post-login analytics is retained

h2. Acceptance Criteria

1. Able to login with new flow using email password

h2. Dependencies

1. \[REACTOR\] Login page (submit OTP) – UI
2. \[FUSION\]\[DELIVERY/USER\] Login with email password endpoint
3. \[FUSION\]\[DELIVERY/USER\] Generate OTP endpoint
4. \[FUSION\]\[DELIVERY/USER\] Verify OTP endpoint

3. If false, update the user to emailVerified = true

h2. Context  
we need to enable passwordless sign in through our web

h2. Todo

1. Details:

* Hit POST /auth/v1/magic-link
* Store the email in local storage
* After user redirected to dashboard from magic link, try signing in using email stored in local storage

    * If no email in local storage, show modal to re-fill email
    
* On successful login

    * Hit POST /auth/v1/reset-failed-login-counter
    

2. Reference:

* [\[SPIKE\]\[20250219\] Magic Link Login](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1PmcIjTSlQhFo6VB21E4z3qLN2Z2FSNpd49B_BUza4MQ/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.6jynaot9cbnq)
* [https://github.com/Match-Made/mm-web/pull/593](https://github.com/Match-Made/mm-web/pull/593)

h2. Acceptance Criteria

1. Able to login using magic link through reactor

h2. Dependencies

1. \[FUSION\]\[DELIVERY/USER\] Reset failed login counter endpoint
2. \[FUSION\]\[DELIVERY/USER\] Magic link login endpoint
3. \[REACTOR\] Magic link login – UI

h2. Context  
need to get user details based on uid

h2. Todo

1. Endpoint: GET /api/v1/users/:id
2. Response:  
  {  
   "id": “”  
   "name": “”,"email": “”,"type": “”,"is_active": “”,"company_name": “”,"phone_number": “”  
  }
3. Should also return the user’s

h2. Acceptance Criteria

1. Able to get user details accordingly

h2. Dependencies

1. \[FUSION\] Migration and package initialization

h2. Context  
need to be able to update user details

h2. Todo

1. Endpoint: PATCH /api/v1/users/:id
2. Request:  
  {  
   "verification": {  
     "current_password": “”,"otp": “”,"token_id": “”  
   },  
   "updated_data": {  
     "phone_number": “”,"password": “”,"last_password_reminder_at": “”  
   }  
  }
3. Response: { user object }
4. Verification object

* current_password: used to try signing in to check legitimacy
* token_id: used to get stored otp
* otp: used to compare with the stored otp

5. On change phone number

* All verification fields are required

    * Try signing in using current_password
    * Try comparing the submitted otp with the stored otp (get auth:otp:{token_id})
    
* Update phone_number_changed_at
* Will use 62 (or +62) → based off of SPIKE

6. On change password

* Only current_password field is required in verification

    * Try signing in using current_password
    
* Hit holaclient UpdateUser
* Update password_changed_at
* If successfully updated, call RevokeRefreshTokens function to force log out

7. Updates can be partial

h2. Acceptance Criteria

1. Able to update user details accordingly

h2. Dependencies

1. \[FUSION\] Migration and package initialization
2. \[FUSION\] Verify Login OTP endpoint

2. Should include:

* Entry point, account page, password modal, WA modal
* Microinteractions
* Error states + form validation
* Confirmation modal when leaving WA modal

3. Add +62 as prefix for phone number
4. Make sure this is only shown to user-typed user

h2. Acceptance Criteria

1. Styling and microinteractions are according to Figma

h2. Dependencies

1. 

    *

h2. Context  
we need to be able to update user account from web

h2. Todo

1. Show user info in the account page

* Hit GET /users/:id

2. Upon requesting OTP

* Hit POST /auth/v1/otp

    * Use phone_number
    * If user re-request OTP, make sure to use the firstly received token_id from this endpoint in request body
    

3. Upon changing phone number

* Hit PATCH /users/:id
* Fill the verification object

    * current_password
    * OTP
    * token_id (from [c.ii](http://c.ii))
    

4. Upon changing password

* Hit PATCH /users/:id
* Fill the verification object

    * current_password
    

5. Make sure this is only shown to user-typed user

h2. Acceptance Criteria

1. Able to change password
2. Able to change phone number and verify it

h2. Dependencies

1. \[FUSION\]\[DELIVERY/USER\] Get user detail endpoint
2. \[FUSION\]\[DELIVERY/USER\] Update user endpoint
3. \[FUSION\]\[DELIVERY/USER\] Generate OTP endpoint

fields.summary: [REACTOR] Update Password reminder pop up modal – Integration
fields.description:
h2. Context  
need to know when the last time a user has been updated

h2. Todo

1. Upon opening web/after logging in, hit GET /users/:id
2. If last_password_reminder_at is >= 90 days ago, show modal
3. This modal will persist like TnC until user clicks “Remind me later”
4. Upon clicking “Update password”

* Hit PATCH /users/:id

    * last_password_reminder_at = today
    
* Redirect to account detail page with password change pane opened

5. On click “remind me later”

* Hit PATCH /users/:id

    * last_password_reminder_at = today
    

6. Make sure this is only shown to user-typed user

h2. Acceptance Criteria

1. TBD

h2. Dependencies

1. \[REACTOR\] Password reminder pop up modal – UI
2. \[REACTOR\] Account detail - UI
3. \[FUSION\] Get user detail endpoint
4. \[FUSION\] Update user endpoint

4. Make sure we’re able to handle case when cloudflare is down so it doesn’t block login flow
